# BEVPlace: Learning LiDAR-based Place Recognition using Bird's Eye View Images

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** KITTI Odometry 00, 02, 05 and 06, ALITA, PointNetVLAD benchmark Oxford, U.S., R.A. and B.D. — `source_fact`; manual, p. 5–7, § Abstract;III-IV. BEVPlace;V. Experiments, Fig. 2-8;Table I-IV
- **input_unit:** single LiDAR scan cropped to a 40 m cube, usually downsampled to 4096 points and projected to BEV — `source_fact`; manual, p. 2–5, § Abstract;III-IV. BEVPlace;V. Experiments, Fig. 2-8;Table I-IV
- **invariances:** planar yaw rotation under the SO(2) BEV model, no translation invariance — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 3–5, § Abstract;III-IV. BEVPlace;V. Experiments, Fig. 2-8;Table I-IV
- **representation:** 0.4 m point-density bird's-eye-view image, SO(2) group-convolution local features, NetVLAD rotation-invariant global descriptor, lazy triplet loss, feature-distance-to-geometric-distance mapping and multilateration-style position estimator — `source_fact`; manual, p. 2–5, § Abstract;III-IV. BEVPlace;V. Experiments, Fig. 2-8;Table I-IV
- **uses_learning:** yes — `source_fact`; manual, p. 1–9, § Abstract;III-IV. BEVPlace;V. Experiments, Fig. 2-8;Table I-IV
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–9, § Abstract;III-IV. BEVPlace;V. Experiments, Fig. 2-8;Table I-IV
